expectations.text: It says nested `asyncio.run()` under a running loop is invalid.
expectations.evidence: Line 1: 'asyncio.run() inside library methods is a boundary bug, not a wrapper strategy.' Line 3: 'It is illegal in a thread that already has a running loop.'

expectations.text: It distinguishes AnyIO worker threads from foreign threads.
expectations.evidence: Lines 52-60: worker thread callback using from_thread.run_sync(done.set). Lines 103-117: foreign thread example using current_token() with explicit label 'For foreign threads, use a token for one-off hops, or a portal for repeated structured access.'

expectations.text: It introduces `BlockingPortal` or token-based access for sync-to-async bridging.
expectations.evidence: Line 12: 'from anyio.from_thread import BlockingPortal' used in SyncClient pattern. Line 107: 'current_token()' for foreign thread one-off access. Lines 119-131: explicit guidance on when BlockingPortal is the right tool.

expectations.text: It states that `from_thread.run()` / `from_thread.run_sync()` work directly only from AnyIO worker threads spawned via `to_thread.run_sync()`.
expectations.evidence: Lines 103-104: 'For foreign threads, use a token for one-off hops, or a portal for repeated structured access' implying from_thread APIs alone are not sufficient for foreign threads. The worker thread examples at lines 55-57 show from_thread.run_sync used in AnyIO worker context.

expectations.text: It distinguishes `from_thread.run()` for async callables from `from_thread.run_sync()` for loop-thread sync callbacks such as setting an AnyIO `Event`.
expectations.evidence: Line 60: 'Use from_thread.run_sync() there, not from_thread.run(), because event.set is a sync loop-thread callback.' Line 76: 'Use from_thread.run() only for async callables.'

expectations.text: It mentions `current_token()` as the lighter one-off bridge for foreign threads.
expectations.evidence: Lines 107-108: 'from anyio.lowlevel import current_token' used in foreign thread example. Worker thread callback uses token parameter at line 110: 'from_thread.run_sync(done.set, token=token)'

expectations.text: It mentions `from_thread.check_cancelled()` for cooperative cancellation in long-running worker-thread code.
expectations.evidence: Lines 84-88: 'def long_sync_job() -> None: while True: from_thread.check_cancelled() do_one_chunk()' with lines 99-101: 'prompt stopping requires cooperative polling with from_thread.check_cancelled()'

expectations.text: It does not recommend `BlockingPortal` for a one-off callback from an AnyIO worker thread when `from_thread.run()` or `from_thread.run_sync()` is enough.
expectations.evidence: Lines 126-131: 'When it is not: a one-off call from an AnyIO worker thread, a worker-thread callback that only needs event.set()'

expectations.text: It does not promise that cancelling the caller will stop arbitrary worker-thread code.
expectations.evidence: Lines 99-101: 'abandon_on_cancel=True does not stop the thread / it only stops waiting for the result / prompt stopping requires cooperative polling with from_thread.check_cancelled()'
